en.value:
Identify the needs and acquisitions for selected five ESCs.
	Develop an intensive communications plan, including designing, developing, disseminating posters, flyers and broadcasting radio jingles.  
	Conduct campaigns to promote service centres' services in collaboration with social partners, vocational training institutes, higher secondary schools and universities.
	Identify training needs, design and conduct training for ESC staff.
	Adaptation and translation of the ILO’s Course and Facilitator’s Guide on Employment Services.
	Organise visits for the staff to learn from successful and relevant experiences in the region.
	Design a computer-based system on jobseekers, vacancies and job placements.
	Review the existing policy and legislations and identify the need for regulation and policy changes for the collection and use of skills indicators.
	Identify the platforms and data formats for interfaces between the different systems, develop required data transfer/exchange protocols and develop the required interfaces.
	Document, publish and share good practices and lessons learned on employment service provision and disseminate them amongst the ESCs and social partner.

executing_agency: Ministry of Labour, Employment and Social Security

en.full:
Skills for Employment and Productivity in Low-Income Countries
Labour Market Information and Employment Services (LIfE) Project

sectors.donor_sector: General Economic Commercial and Labor Affairs
sectors.normalized_sector: General Economic Commercial and Labor Affairs

version_summary.change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API
